author: @JohnSmith-zq2dr
text: This is so practical. How to make milk from your own nuts! I'm a vegan and around here many of the supermarkets have reduced their stock of soymilk and are frequently out of stock and they seem to be using more thickener which as you mentioned comes from the sea. This is awesome. Thank's man!

author: @shreddedreams
text: Robert Murray-Smith  Hi, so how thick is this compared to standard whole (full fat milk) ?

author: @shreddedreams
text: This is actually brilliant. I have obviously used almond and hazel, but never though of peanuts or cashews!

replies.text: +zerpBot Cow milk is harmful for humans. You should find alternative.

replies.author: @zer0b0t
replies.text:
Dino Sabanovic
I've never heard that one, but I'll investigate anyways, thanks.

replies.author: @plankton50
replies.text: +zerpBot If you got a calcium supplement you could  put it in your blender or just take the supplement. Studies have shown that the added calcium in fake milks is absorbed by the body just as well as cows milk calcium and all they're doing is adding calcium carbonate which is exactly the same thing you would find in a supplement. Also B12.

author: @Lukegear
text: I never even heard of nut milk other than coconut milk, that's actually quite interesting. I'm from Brazil, so I wonder if it's a good idea to try that with brazilnuts being that they are very nutriente rich (minerals, vitamins, the list goes on). What do you think ?

author: @strongforce8466
text:
I'm a huge fan of almond milk !  but yea it's kinda watered down, though the non watered down (thick) wouldn't suit me for my cornflakes lol !

Also if you buy the almonds/nuts separately, wouldn't that cost more ?

I'll have to try.. hemp ?? :O, how do you filter the harder parts just a strainer I guess

replies.author: @AshleyMillsTube
replies.text:
Thomas Syxe you can get 1kg of cashews here for around 9 pounds. If you use 100g per litre of milk, that's 90p a litre. In the shops here nutmilk is about 1.50 per litre or more, so it is cheaper to make it. They also don't sell cashew milk in the supermarket.

With the hemp you need to use hemp hearts which are the de-shelled hemp seeds. This is a pity as they cost loads more as soon as they are de-shelled. I bought some previously from rawliving.eu

replies.author: @shreddedreams
replies.text: Ashley Mills To be fair though in the Uk quality nuts are very expensive and 100grams of nuts per litre will usually cost 1.50 even for loq quality nuts. Cashews being one of the cheapest of course, Unless you try peanut which I will try soon, but even still in terms of protein and nutrients milk is far more cost effective **

replies.author: @AshleyMillsTube
replies.text: If you are willing to do without organic, you can get 1kg for 8 quid in my local shop. It's funny, since i started drinking it I seem to care less about how milky it is. I think a lot is the psychology from years of drinking cows milk as it does a great job of making things look milky. But after drinking nut milk for 6 months I don't really care anymore if it makes things look milky. Still find it hard to make a good latte though

replies.author: @shreddedreams
replies.text:
I see. well even if I could get the nuts at those prices.. by the time the kilo is used up they nuts would go rancid.. (presuming they are packed in nitrogen) unless I freeze the milk I make in bulk..

Have you tried combining coconut milk with nut milk to increase the thickness?

there is a company that makes excellent milk. Too thick to drink (though I have) but I suspect a 50/50 mix would be perfect with cashew*

http://www.amazon.co.uk/gp/product/B00CPMJCAK?psc=1&redirect=true&ref_=oh_aui_search_detailpage

descent price too considering it is 75% and no fillers

replies.author: @strongforce8466
replies.text: Mmh I need to try coconut milk myself

author: @johnnydoe6325
text:
I've made nut milk before...Pretty good but wasn't the most cost effective for me.
So I switched to making Rice,and Oat milk.
Have you ever tried them,Oat is my favorite because it's the easiest to make : )

replies.author: @AshleyMillsTube
replies.text: Johnny Doe I tried rice from the supermarket and was surprised how not-bad it tasted, I imagined it was going to taste like some old takeaway but it was actually nice.  I wanted something that replaces the protein in milk which I why I went for nuts but I will have to try the oat one just out of curiosity.
